title: Choose Absolute Units from Output Medium
summary: Build and verify css absolute units in screen and printed relief-pass sizing review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css absolute units from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css absolute units without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the screen and printed relief-pass sizing review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves css absolute units when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: screen and printed relief-pass sizing review.
steps.why: Choose Absolute Units from Output Medium makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in screen and printed relief-pass sizing review.

content.text: CSS absolute units include px, in, cm, mm, q, pt, and pc. On screens, a CSS pixel is a reference unit and may map to multiple physical pixels depending on device scale and zoom. Physical units are most meaningful in controlled print output. Unit choice follows the output medium, user zoom, and the thing being measured—not a blanket rule that pixels are inaccessible. Apply it to screen and printed relief-pass sizing review.
